zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Clare於1292年10月3日出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根凱爾費利 卡素時，她的父親Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester49歲，母親Countess Joan of Acre20歲。她於1306年6月14日與Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1337年6月30日在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利享年44歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor de Clare于1292年10月3日出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根凱爾費利 卡素时，她的父亲Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester49岁，母亲Countess Joan of Acre20岁。她于1306年6月14日与Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser在英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1337年6月30日在英国格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利享年44岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Clare was born on 3 October 1292, in Caerphilly Castle, Glamorgan, Wales, her father, Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester, was 49 and her mother, Countess Joan of Acre, was 20. She married Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser on 14 June 1306, in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 30 June 1337, in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor de Clare est née le 3 octobre 1292, à Caerphilly Castle, Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Countess Joan of Acre, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser le 14 juin 1306, à Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 juin 1337, à Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor de Clare am 3. Oktober 1292 in Caerphilly Castle, Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester 49 und ihre Mutter Countess Joan of Acre 20. Sie hat am 14. Juni 1306 in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. Juni 1337 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Clare è nata il il 3 ottobre 1292 nel Caerphilly Castle, Glamorgan, Galles, sua padre, Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester, aveva 49 anni e sua madre, Countess Joan of Acre, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser il 14 giugno 1306, nel Westminster, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 30 giugno 1337, nel Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Clareが1292年10月3日にウェールズグラモーガンケエルフィリー カストルで生まれた時，父Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucesterは49歳，母Countess Joan of Acreは20歳であった。1306年6月14日にイギリスイギリスロンドンウエストミンスターでSir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenserと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1337年6月30日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーで44歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーテウケスブリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Tewkesbury Abbey, Gloucestershire, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이
ko.Westminster Abbey, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Clare(이)가 1292년 10월 3일 웨일스 글램르건 카이르필리 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Countess Joan of Acre(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1306년 6월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터에서 Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1337년 6월 30일 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Clare nasceu em 3 de outubro de 1292, em Caerphilly Castle, Glamorgan, Gales, seu pai, Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Countess Joan of Acre, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser em 14 de junho de 1306, em Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Clare родилась 3 октября 1292 г. в местности каирфилли кастл, глэморган, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Countess Joan of Acre, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Hugh le Despenser 1st Lord Despenser 14 июня 1306 г. в местности вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 30 июня 1337 г. в местности тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия.
